message:
fix(fcp-host): coalesce concurrent same-zone handshakes (br-j1pjg)

crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:240-283 — SubprocessConnector
::ensure_handshake previously acquired `handshaken_zone.lock().await`,
read its content, DROPPED the guard, awaited the handshake RPC, then
re-acquired to write. Two concurrent same-zone invokes on a
just-configured connector could both see `None`, both issue the
handshake RPC, and both overwrite handshaken_zone — minting two
SessionIds on the connector side and burning external handshake
budget before the first real invoke ran.

Fix: hold the same MutexGuard across the entire ensure_handshake
flow. Concurrent callers serialize at `lock().await`; the first
performs the RPC while holding the lock, the second acquires,
sees the cached zone, and returns `Ok(())` without a second RPC.
Single-connector serialization has negligible cost (one RPC round
trip) and eliminates the race entirely.

Regression test: subprocess_connector_concurrent_same_zone_invokes_
all_succeed fires 8 concurrent invokes against a single connector
under FCP_TEST_CONNECTOR_REQUIRE_HANDSHAKE and asserts all complete
with InvokeStatus::Ok + no deadlock (5-second timeout). A true
RPC-count assertion (exactly one handshake) requires a connector-side
handshake counter, filed as follow-up flywheel_connectors-4mjap on
fcp-test-connector.

Verified:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline -p fcp-host \
    --bin fcp-host handshake
  -> 5 passed; 0 failed (4 pre-existing + new concurrent test).

Closes flywheel_connectors-j1pjg.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
